_meta.description: Core fact validation requirements for extraction, citation grounding, and field typing.

rules.trigger: Numeric fact has no unit or currency while context indicates money/tax/weight/time.
rules.check: fact.type == 'numeric' AND fact.kind IN ['amount', 'balance', 'rate', 'tax', 'fee', 'quantity', 'quantity_value'] AND (fact.unit == null || fact.currency == null)
examples: Source: 120,000 in revenue table without unit or currency metadata.
Source: 1,500 with no currency context in financial statement row.

rules.trigger: Fact is period-sensitive but period is absent from extracted evidence.
examples: Q3 revenue with no period label in source row.
Tax line item extracted without reporting period.
Counterparty row extracted without legal name.
Supplier and vendor identities mixed in summary.

rules.trigger: Parsed numeric fact outside allowed magnitude for declared type.
rules.check: fact.type == 'numeric' AND fact.value != null AND fact.expectedRange != null AND (fact.value < fact.expectedRange.min OR fact.value > fact.expectedRange.max)
examples: Balance reported as -999999999 for cash item without scale note.
Percent stored as 9000 instead of 90 for 90%.

rules.trigger: Fact value depends on ambiguous merged table cell with no row/column context.
examples: Merged row header used as amount without line context.
Table heading used as fact value.

templates.text: Use machine-actionable policy fallback when fact type rule is missing.
